title: artemisia vulgaris subsp. wrightii
intro: Artemisia vulgaris subsp. wrightii, also known as Wright's mugwort, is a subspecies of the common mugwort plant, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antihelmintic properties. The health benefits of Wright's mugwort include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve menstrual cramps, and support the immune system. This plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, from gastrointestinal problems to skin conditions.
